{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Radio / TV Studio Manager",
  "description": "Natafamdavid Consulting Nigeria Limited is a business growth consultancy company offering tailor made services targeted at building profitable and productive companies with a focus on building sustainable enterprises, comprehensive HR advisory services, brand growth, corporate governance, business strategy and advisory services at different sections of the value chain.

Our services are across-the-board and we are driven by a strong passion to create value for our clients. From the het go, we set out to work with clients across diverse industries, providing strategic solutions to increase our success matrix, productivity, profitability. So far, we have worked with clients in different industries and sectors such as Agriculture, Real Estate, Hospitality, Engineering &amp; Construction, Education, Logistics, Health &amp; Environment, Oil &amp; gas, Retail and more, to achieve their itemized objectives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Radio / TV Studio Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Manage and supervise daily studio operations for TV and Radio broadcasts.
	Coordinate with producers, presenters, engineers, and technical crew during live and recorded programs.
	Ensure all studio equipment is properly set up and functioning before broadcasts.
	Maintain order, discipline, and professional standards within the studio environment.
	Schedule studio usage for programs, recordings, and rehearsals.
	Ensure compliance with safety and operational procedures.
	Report technical issues and liaise with engineering teams for prompt resolution.
	Support program production teams to achieve high-quality broadcast output.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND / BSc in Mass Communication, Broadcasting, Media Technology, or related field.
	3&ndash;5 years of experience in studio operations within a TV or Radio station.
	Good knowledge of broadcast studio equipment and operations.
	Strong organizational and coordination skills.
	Ability to work under pressure, especially during live broadcasts.
	Good communication and leadership abilities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;nadrecruitmentng@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-03-14",

  },

Radio / TV Studio Manager Job at Natafamdavid Consulting Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Natafamdavid Consulting Nigeria Limited is a business growth consultancy company offering tailor made services targeted at building profitable and productive companies with a focus on building sustainable enterprises, comprehensive HR advisory services, brand growth, corporate governance, business strategy and advisory services at different sections of the value chain.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our services are across-the-board and we are driven by a strong passion to create value for our clients. From the het go, we set out to work with clients across diverse industries, providing strategic solutions to increase our success matrix, productivity, profitability. So far, we have worked with clients in different industries and sectors such as Agriculture, Real Estate, Hospitality, Engineering & Construction, Education, Logistics, Health & Environment, Oil & gas, Retail and more, to achieve their itemized objectives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Radio / TV Studio Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Manage and supervise daily studio operations for TV and Radio broadcasts.
	Coordinate with producers, presenters, engineers, and technical crew during live and recorded programs.
	Ensure all studio equipment is properly set up and functioning before broadcasts.
	Maintain order, discipline, and professional standards within the studio environment.
	Schedule studio usage for programs, recordings, and rehearsals.
	Ensure compliance with safety and operational procedures.
	Report technical issues and liaise with engineering teams for prompt resolution.
	Support program production teams to achieve high-quality broadcast output.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND / BSc in Mass Communication, Broadcasting, Media Technology, or related field.
	3–5 years of experience in studio operations within a TV or Radio station.
	Good knowledge of broadcast studio equipment and operations.
	Strong organizational and coordination skills.
	Ability to work under pressure, especially during live broadcasts.
	Good communication and leadership abilities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: nadrecruitmentng@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Officer",
  "description": "Ascentech Services Limited is the most sought-after Human Resource Solution Provider with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We have a reputation for satisfying and surpassing clients expectations through our core offerings: Recruitment and Selection, Outsourcing, Training and Development. To accomplish these we leverage on creating and managing change in organizations by improving efficiency through our proven and dynamic HR solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Oniru, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Level: Middle Management

Role Summary


	The Human Resources Officer will support HR operations including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, training, and HR administration while ensuring compliance with company policies and labor regulations.


Key Responsibilities
Recruitment &amp; Staffing:


	Manage end-to-end recruitment from job posting to onboarding.
	Screen candidates, coordinate interviews, and conduct background checks.
	Recruit operational staff such as spa therapists, nail technicians, and attendants.
	Maintain a talent pipeline and support smooth onboarding.


Employee Relations:


	Address employee concerns and support conflict resolution.
	Assist with employee engagement and workplace wellness initiatives.
	Support recognition of employee milestones and achievements.


Performance Management:


	Coordinate performance reviews and documentation.
	Assist with performance improvement and employee development plans.


Training &amp; Development:


	Identify training needs and coordinate development programs.
	Monitor training effectiveness and recommend improvements.


HR Administration &amp; Compliance:


	Maintain accurate employee records and HR documentation.
	Manage leave records and HR communications.
	Ensure HR processes comply with company policies and labor laws.


Key Performance Indicators


	Time-to-hire for operational roles
	Quality of hires
	Accuracy of employee records
	Onboarding success rate


Requirements


	2&ndash;3 years of experience in HR
	Experience in recruitment and employee relations preferred
	Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and Canva.


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;cv@ascentech.com.ng&nbsp;using&nbsp;&ldquo;Human Resources Officer&rdquo;&nbsp;as the subject of the email.",

Human Resource Officer Job at Ascentech Services Limited




        
    

        
        Ascentech Services Limited is the most sought-after Human Resource Solution Provider with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We have a reputation for satisfying and surpassing clients expectations through our core offerings: Recruitment and Selection, Outsourcing, Training and Development. To accomplish these we leverage on creating and managing change in organizations by improving efficiency through our proven and dynamic HR solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location: Oniru, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Level: Middle Management

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Human Resources Officer will support HR operations including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, training, and HR administration while ensuring compliance with company policies and labor regulations.


Key Responsibilities
Recruitment & Staffing:


	Manage end-to-end recruitment from job posting to onboarding.
	Screen candidates, coordinate interviews, and conduct background checks.
	Recruit operational staff such as spa therapists, nail technicians, and attendants.
	Maintain a talent pipeline and support smooth onboarding.


Employee Relations:


	Address employee concerns and support conflict resolution.
	Assist with employee engagement and workplace wellness initiatives.
	Support recognition of employee milestones and achievements.


Performance Management:


	Coordinate performance reviews and documentation.
	Assist with performance improvement and employee development plans.


Training & Development:


	Identify training needs and coordinate development programs.
	Monitor training effectiveness and recommend improvements.


HR Administration & Compliance:


	Maintain accurate employee records and HR documentation.
	Manage leave records and HR communications.
	Ensure HR processes comply with company policies and labor laws.


Key Performance Indicators


	Time-to-hire for operational roles
	Quality of hires
	Accuracy of employee records
	Onboarding success rate


Requirements


	2–3 years of experience in HR
	Experience in recruitment and employee relations preferred
	Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and Canva.


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: cv@ascentech.com.ng using “Human Resources Officer” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Manager",
  "description": "TECO is a longstanding Nigerian Engineering firm, founded in 1980. We excel in delivering tailored engineering solutions to a wide range of industries throughout Nigeria and the rest of Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Finance Manager will be responsible for financial planning, budgeting, financial reporting, cost control, tax compliance, and internal financial controls across the organisation.
	The role will also support project financial monitoring, ensuring that fabrication and installation projects remain financially viable and properly accounted for.
	The successful candidate will work closely with management, project teams, procurement, and operations to ensure strong financial governance and profitability.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Management &amp; Reporting:


	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements.
	Maintain accurate general ledger, financial records, and accounting systems.
	Provide financial analysis and reports to management for strategic decisions.
	Ensure compliance with accounting standards and financial regulations.


Budgeting &amp; Cost Control:


	Develop and monitor annual budgets and departmental budgets.
	Track project costs and profitability for palm oil mill fabrication and installation projects.
	Implement systems to control operational and project costs.
	Monitor cash flow and financial performance.


Tax &amp; Regulatory Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with tax regulations (PAYE, VAT, Withholding Tax, Company Income Tax).
	Coordinate statutory filings and regulatory reporting.
	Liaise with external auditors, tax consultants, and regulatory authorities.


Financial Controls &amp; Risk Management:


	Establish and enforce internal financial control systems.
	Monitor procurement and payment processes to ensure financial discipline.
	Identify and mitigate financial risks affecting operations.


Project Financial Oversight:


	Work closely with project managers and operations teams to monitor project budgets.
	Review project cost estimates and financial feasibility.
	Ensure proper financial documentation for project expenditures.


Team Leadership:


	Supervise and guide accounting and finance staff.
	Support training and development within the finance function.
	Promote a culture of financial accountability across the organisation.


Requirements
Education:


	
	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline.
	


Professional Qualification:


	
	ICAN, ACCA, or equivalent professional certification (required).
	


Experience:


	5&ndash;8 years of accounting / finance experience.
	Experience in engineering, manufacturing, construction, or project-based organisations will be an advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong financial analysis and reporting skills
	Knowledge of tax laws and statutory compliance
	Budgeting and cost control expertise
	Strong Excel and accounting software proficiency
	Excellent leadership and communication skills.


Salary
N500,000 - N700,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to:&nbsp;hr@tecogroupng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position&nbsp; as the subject of the email.",

Finance Manager Job at Teco Group




        
    

        
        TECO is a longstanding Nigerian Engineering firm, founded in 1980. We excel in delivering tailored engineering solutions to a wide range of industries throughout Nigeria and the rest of Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Finance Manager will be responsible for financial planning, budgeting, financial reporting, cost control, tax compliance, and internal financial controls across the organisation.
	The role will also support project financial monitoring, ensuring that fabrication and installation projects remain financially viable and properly accounted for.
	The successful candidate will work closely with management, project teams, procurement, and operations to ensure strong financial governance and profitability.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Management & Reporting:


	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements.
	Maintain accurate general ledger, financial records, and accounting systems.
	Provide financial analysis and reports to management for strategic decisions.
	Ensure compliance with accounting standards and financial regulations.


Budgeting & Cost Control:


	Develop and monitor annual budgets and departmental budgets.
	Track project costs and profitability for palm oil mill fabrication and installation projects.
	Implement systems to control operational and project costs.
	Monitor cash flow and financial performance.


Tax & Regulatory Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with tax regulations (PAYE, VAT, Withholding Tax, Company Income Tax).
	Coordinate statutory filings and regulatory reporting.
	Liaise with external auditors, tax consultants, and regulatory authorities.


Financial Controls & Risk Management:


	Establish and enforce internal financial control systems.
	Monitor procurement and payment processes to ensure financial discipline.
	Identify and mitigate financial risks affecting operations.


Project Financial Oversight:


	Work closely with project managers and operations teams to monitor project budgets.
	Review project cost estimates and financial feasibility.
	Ensure proper financial documentation for project expenditures.


Team Leadership:


	Supervise and guide accounting and finance staff.
	Support training and development within the finance function.
	Promote a culture of financial accountability across the organisation.


Requirements
Education:


	
	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline.
	


Professional Qualification:


	
	ICAN, ACCA, or equivalent professional certification (required).
	


Experience:


	5–8 years of accounting / finance experience.
	Experience in engineering, manufacturing, construction, or project-based organisations will be an advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong financial analysis and reporting skills
	Knowledge of tax laws and statutory compliance
	Budgeting and cost control expertise
	Strong Excel and accounting software proficiency
	Excellent leadership and communication skills.


Salary
N500,000 - N700,000 per month. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: hr@tecogroupng.com using the Job Position  as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: Operations Manager - Logistics

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	We are&nbsp;currently seeking qualified and experienced applicants to fill the role of Operations Manager- Logistics&nbsp;for one of our clients.


Responsibilities


	Coordinate with clients, vendors, and internal teams to ensure timely order fulfillment and issue resolution.
	Supervise fleet utilization, maintenance planning, and driver performance to ensure operational efficiency and safety compliance.
	Plan, manage, and monitor end-to-end logistics operations including transportation, distribution, and delivery schedules.
	Implement process improvements to reduce costs, increase productivity, and enhance service quality.
	Monitor operational KPIs, prepare performance reports, and provide strategic recommendations to management.
	Ensure compliance with regulatory, safety, and company policies across all operations.
	Lead, train, and motivate operations staff to achieve departmental goals.
	Manage budgets, resource allocation, and operational risk within the logistics unit.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4&ndash;6 years of experience in Logistics or Operations management (managerial experience preferred).
	Strong leadership, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
	Excellent organizational and communication abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and logistics/transport management systems.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target-driven environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Operations Manager - Logistics Job at Swift Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Swift Consulting Limited - Our client is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager - Logistics

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are currently seeking qualified and experienced applicants to fill the role of Operations Manager- Logistics for one of our clients.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Coordinate with clients, vendors, and internal teams to ensure timely order fulfillment and issue resolution.
	Supervise fleet utilization, maintenance planning, and driver performance to ensure operational efficiency and safety compliance.
	Plan, manage, and monitor end-to-end logistics operations including transportation, distribution, and delivery schedules.
	Implement process improvements to reduce costs, increase productivity, and enhance service quality.
	Monitor operational KPIs, prepare performance reports, and provide strategic recommendations to management.
	Ensure compliance with regulatory, safety, and company policies across all operations.
	Lead, train, and motivate operations staff to achieve departmental goals.
	Manage budgets, resource allocation, and operational risk within the logistics unit.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4–6 years of experience in Logistics or Operations management (managerial experience preferred).
	Strong leadership, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
	Excellent organizational and communication abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and logistics/transport management systems.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target-driven environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hireme@swiftconsulting.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Buymore
content:
Buymore is a retail/supermarket with four branches (GRA Ikeja, Abule Egba, Isheri, & Ibafo).

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Shop Floor Attendant

Job Locations: Ikeja - Lagos and Opic Isheri - Ogun

Responsibilities
Customer Service:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Facilitate the Buymore Brand in ensuring that all customers are welcomed and that they receive the best possible service by:
	Greeting customers and ascertaining what each customer wants or needs.
	Providing a proper level of service to customers and potential customers
	Facilitating sales to customers in compliance with all company policies and conditions
	Maintain knowledge of current sales and promotions, policies regarding payment and exchanges
	Answer customers’ questions about specific products/services


Floor Management:


	Organizing the shop floor and ensuring all merchandize are well displayed on the shelves
	Ensure racks are fully stocked at all times.
	Ensure all products on display are tagged with current prices
	Daily clean shelves, merchandize and tables.
	Watch for and recognize security risks and thefts, and know how to prevent or handle these situations.


Operational Execution:


	Ensure compliance with all operating standards, policies, and procedures, which include:
	Ensuring the safekeeping and protection of all company assets (owned or rented)
	Replenishing sold items from the stock room
	Inform procurement departments of products on demand by customers
	Notify Inventory department about products expiring date
	Provide customer feedback to the Store Manager.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: comfort.achi@buymore.com.ng using the Job Position and location as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Cashier

Job Locations: Ikeja - Lagos and Opic Isheri - Ogun

Responsibilities


	Uses the cash desk, Till and back-office systems of the shop in strict compliance with Buymore’s procedures, which include:
	
		Processing all sales transactions
		Capturing customer details for any loyalty schemes
		Processing all sales return transactions (approved by the Supervisor or the General Supervisor)
		Checking the availability of stock of products for customer requests.
	
	


Customer Service:


	Ensuring that all customers are welcomed and that they receive the best possible service at all times.
	Greets and receives customers in a welcoming manner.
	Respond to customers’ questions and direct to the Supervisor on duty if you are unable to provide a satisfactory response.


Execution of policies and Procedures:


	Executes all operating standards, policies, and procedures of the organization


Other Responsibilities:


	Ensure your work area is always tidy and presentable
	Champions the Buymore Brand and Values
	Champions the Standard Operating Procedure of Buymore
	Reconciliation of the previous day's transactions with the account
	Cashing out at the appropriate time according to Buymore’s policy.
	Performs other duties and responsibilities as assigned by the management.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree or an OND qualification.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: comfort.achi@buymore.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) IT Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The IT Officer is responsible for managing and maintaining the company’s IT infrastructure, systems, network, and technical support services.
	The role ensures optimal system performance, data security, business continuity, and efficient use of technology resources across the organization.


Key Responsibilities
IT Infrastructure & Systems Management:


	Install, configure, and maintain computer systems, printers, scanners, and other peripherals.
	Monitor and maintain network systems (LAN/WAN, routers, switches, firewalls).
	Ensure uninterrupted internet connectivity across branches.
	Oversee server management, system backups, and recovery processes.
	Maintain CCTV systems and access control systems (where applicable).


Technical Support:


	Provide first-level technical support to staff.
	Troubleshoot hardware and software issues promptly.
	Maintain an IT helpdesk log for tracking issues and resolutions.
	Ensure timely resolution of user complaints.


Data Security & Compliance:


	Implement and monitor cybersecurity measures.
	Ensure regular system updates and antivirus protection.
	Manage user access controls and password policies.
	Safeguard company data and ensure confidentiality.


IT Policy & Governance:


	Enforce IT and Acceptable Use Policies.
	Monitor internet usage and prevent unauthorized access.
	Recommend improvements to IT policies and procedures.


Vendor & Asset Management:


	Liaise with internet service providers and IT vendors.
	Maintain IT asset register (laptops, desktops, routers, etc.).
	Monitor software licenses and subscriptions.
	Ensure cost-effective procurement of IT equipment.


Business Continuity:


	Perform regular system backups.
	Develop disaster recovery procedures.
	Ensure minimal downtime in case of system failure.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess an HND / Bachelor`s Degree with 3 years of experience.


Application Closing Date: 15th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: comfort.achi@buymore.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
